prompt: Play this short World 3 preflop category-reuse chain.
why_v1: World 3 reuses the same action sequence while the hand bucket and preflop frame shift underneath it.

steps.prompt: Step 1: Hero is on the button with KJs and the pot is unopened. Which compact preflop action fits best?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. A solid suited broadway hand on the button is strong enough to open when nobody entered the pot yet.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. KJs on the button is strong enough to open when nobody entered yet, so raising uses both hand quality and late position.
steps.why_v1: Category first: a stronger playable hand in position should usually open when the pot is still unopened.

steps.prompt: Step 2: Keep the same hand and seats, but now the cutoff opened first. Which simple action fits better for hero on the button?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The open changed the frame, so calling is the cleaner preflop default for this same hand in position.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Once the cutoff opens first, KJs still has enough value to continue, but calling is cleaner than replaying it like an unopened-pot raise.
steps.why_v1: The same hand category can prefer a different first action once the action gets to you in a different preflop frame.

steps.prompt: Step 3: Hero is still on the button, but now the hand is A8o and the cutoff opened first. Which compact action fits best?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. A weaker offsuit hand should not keep going just because the button has position.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. A8o may have an ace, but against an open it is still a weak offsuit continue, so folding is better than leaning too hard on button position.
steps.recap_v1: World 3 gets cleaner when the learner reuses the same simple habit: identify the hand bucket, then let the preflop frame decide whether the hand wants raise, call, or fold.
steps.why_v1: Position matters, but hand quality still decides whether continuing is disciplined or just hopeful.
